_README: Golden cross-language parity cases for the FIFA WC2026 Article 13 group-stage tiebreaker. Consumed by BOTH backend/tests/test_standings_parity.py (pytest) and frontend/src/lib/utils/standings.parity.test.ts (vitest). If you change the tiebreaker on one side, these cases must still pass on both — that is the whole point. Each case is either context 'group_standings' (provide full `group` + `matches`; both sides build standings from the matches then rank) or context 'third_place_qualifying' (provide pre-aggregated `teams` with neutral keys points/gd/gf; H2H is N/A cross-group). `fifa_rankings` is the ordered ranking list (index 0 = rank #1) for Article 13 Step 3. Warnings are compared on (tied_teams sorted, context) only — `group` is intentionally not asserted because it is ambiguous for cross-group ties.

cases.name: group_b_h2h_beats_overall_gd
cases._note: User's real Group B case. CAN & SUI both on 6 pts; SUI beat CAN 1-0 head-to-head, so SUI ranks 1st even though CAN has the better overall GD (+5 vs +1). This is the exact bug that motivated the fix.

cases.name: descent_to_overall_gd_after_h2h_draw
cases._note: ZULU & ALFA both on 4 pts, drew their H2H 1-1 (H2H equal), so the chain descends to overall GD: ZULU +3 beats ALFA +1. Names chosen so the GD winner is alphabetically LAST — proves GD, not alphabetical order, decided it.

cases.name: descent_to_overall_gf_after_gd_tie
cases._note: MIKE & NOVEMBER on 4 pts, H2H 2-2 draw (equal), overall GD both +2 (equal), so chain descends to overall GF: NOVEMBER 6 beats MIKE 5. Winner is alphabetically last — proves GF, not alphabetical.

cases.name: fair_play_then_fifa_rankings_resolve
cases._note: GOLF & HOTEL fully tied (4 pts, H2H 0-0, GD +1, GF 1). Chain reaches fair-play (untracked → warning) then FIFA Rankings. Rankings list HOTEL above GOLF, so HOTEL ranks 1st despite being alphabetically last — proves rankings drove it. JULIETT is alone in 3rd (no tie, no warning).

cases.name: fair_play_unlisted_falls_to_alphabetical
cases._note: WHISKEY & XRAY fully tied (4 pts, H2H 0-0, GD +1, GF 1) and neither is in fifa_rankings, so after the fair-play warning the chain falls to alphabetical: WHISKEY before XRAY.

cases.name: third_place_cross_group_rankings_decide
cases._note: Three third-placed teams from different groups, all identical on points/GD/GF. H2H is N/A cross-group, so the chain goes points → GD → GF → fair-play(warn) → FIFA Rankings. Rankings are reverse-alphabetical, so order follows rankings (GREENVILLE, BLUTOPIA, REDLAND), proving rankings — not alphabetical — drove it.
